meanings.definition: decoration that is hung (as a tapestry) on a wall or over a window
examples: the cold castle walls were covered with hangings

meanings.definition: a form of capital punishment; victim is suspended by the neck from a gallows or gibbet until dead
examples: in those days the hanging of criminals was a public entertainment

meanings.definition: the act of suspending something (hanging it from above so it moves freely)
examples: there was a small ceremony for the hanging of the portrait
The flag hung on the wall

meanings.definition: cause to be hanging or suspended
examples: Hang that picture on the wall
The murderer was hanged on Friday
Hang one's head in shame

meanings.definition: fall or flow in a certain way
examples: This dress hangs well
Her long black hair flowed down her back
This worry hangs on my mind
The cloud of suspicion hangs over her
The children in the audience attended the recital quietly
She hung on his every word
They attended to everything he said
Heavy fog hung over the valley
hang on to your father's hands
The child clung to his mother's apron
Picasso hangs in this new wing of the museum

meanings.definition: prevent from reaching a verdict, of a jury
decorate or furnish with something suspended
examples: Hang wallpaper

meanings.definition: be placed in position as by a hinge
examples: This cabinet door doesn't hang right!

meanings.definition: place in position as by a hinge so as to allow free movement in one direction
examples: hang a door

meanings.definition: suspend (meat) in order to get a gamey taste
examples: hang the venison for a few days
